description_long: This object consists of two upright wooden poles supporting a taut clothesline, from which several pieces of laundry are hung. The laundry items include variously colored and shaped cloths, such as a green towel, a brown sheet, a gray shirt, and dark pants, all draped over the line. The poles are angled slightly outward for stability, and the line appears to be made of a thin, flexible material, likely rope or cord. The laundry items are spaced along the line, each hanging freely and swaying slightly as if exposed to wind. The overall structure is simple and utilitarian, commonly found in residential or communal outdoor spaces for air-drying clothes. The materials and construction suggest it is a temporary, movable installation rather than a permanent fixture.
description: A wooden clothesline with two angled poles and several colorful laundry items hanging from a rope, typical for outdoor drying in urban or residential areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full spread of laundry items hanging between two wooden poles, with all colors and shapes visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the structure, with the laundry items mostly edge-on and the depth of the poles apparent.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the backs of the laundry items and the supporting poles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the alignment of the laundry along the rope.
